title: Fixed-Length, Close-Delimited, Absent, and Incomplete Content · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs fixed-length, close-delimited, absent, and incomplete content from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.
Classify EOF before the required octet count as incomplete framing and prevent dispatch or connection reuse.
Stop after exactly the framed content and retain buffered residual bytes for the next message.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Content Decoding Limit
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 9910 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep message framing separate from content coding and bound both encoded and decoded sizes against expansion attacks.

Inspect the Content Decoding Limit trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Content Decoding Limit.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for keep message framing separate from content coding and bound both encoded and decoded sizes against expansion attacks. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Content Decoding Limit, keep Content Decoding Limit connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
